'''David Ioane Vaealiki''' ({{IPAc-en|v|eɪ|ə|l|ɪ|k|i}}) (born 13 November 1980) is a New Zealand former [[rugby league]] and [[rugby union]] footballer who most recently played for [[SC Albi]]. He has represented New Zealand in [[rugby league]] and previously played in the [[National Rugby League]] in Australia and in the [[Super League]], primarily as a {{rlp|ce}}.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Early years and Parramatta==
A product of New Zealand junior side Sydenham, David Vaealiki attended [[Linwood High School]] and became [[Canterbury Rugby League|Canterbury's]] youngest ever representative when he made his début for the province aged just 17 years and 199 days in a match against the West Coast.<ref>John Coffey [http://www.highbeam.com/doc/1P2-17351318.html Youngsters in Canty side for West Coast fixture ]{{dead link|date=February 2019|bot=medic}}{{cbignore|bot=medic}} ''The Press'', 29 May 1998</ref> He then signed for the [[Parramatta Eels]] and made his first grade début in 1999 against [[Canberra Raiders|Canberra]]. He scored his first try for the club in their 68-10 victory [[Western Suburbs Magpies|Western Suburbs]] in round 20. Vaealiki played six games for Parramatta in his debut season including the clubs preliminary final loss to [[Melbourne Storm|Melbourne]]. During this time he taught Physical Education at Castle Hill High School, where the league team he coached had 0 wins. He went on to play for Parramatta at centre in their shock [[2001 NRL Grand Final]] loss to the [[Newcastle Knights]]. Vaealiki scored the fastest [[hat-trick]] in NRL history against [[Penrith Panthers|Penrith]] on 17 March 2002. In 2004, his season was cut short by an [[Achilles tendon]] injury, he played only eight games.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2018/09/21/finals-footy-flashback-1999-preliminary-final-eels-v-storm/|title=Finals Footy Flashback: 1999 Preliminary Final Eels v Storm|publisher=National Rugby League}}</ref><ref name="theroar.com.au">{{Cite web|url=https://www.theroar.com.au/2012/05/15/so-close-yet-so-far-for-parramatta/|title=So close yet so far for Parramatta}}</ref>

==Wigan Warriors career==
He signed a three-year contract with the Wigan Warriors in July 2004. He joined the club for the 2005 [[Super League]] and his first appearance for Wigan Warriors on 30 January 2005 in a pre-season friendly against the [[London Broncos#1994–2005: Broncos and Super League|London Broncos]] after starting the match from the bench. He made his first start for the Wigan Warriors in their 15–4 win over the [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]] in the [[Super League]] his last game was the semi-final against the [[Leeds Rhinos]] during October 2007.

==Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles==
David Vaealiki was expected to join the [[London Broncos#2006–2011: Harlequins Rugby League|Harlequins RL]] when his [[Wigan Warriors]] contract expired at the end of 2007's [[Super League XII]] and it was not known if he was offered a new contract. During September 2007 he was linked with a move to French [[rugby union]] side [[SC Albi|Sporting Club Albigeois]].

Instead Vaealiki joined the [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]] on a one-year deal and played two games for the club in early 2008.<ref name="Vaealiki signs for the Sea Eagles">{{cite web|url=http://www.manlyseaeagles.com.au/news.asp?tx=39&newsid=2739|title=Vaealiki signs for the Sea Eagles|publisher=ManlySeaEagles.com|date=26 November 2007|accessdate=26 November 2007|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20071201125412/http://www.manlyseaeagles.com.au/news.asp?tx=39&newsid=2739|archivedate=1 December 2007|df=dmy-all}}</ref>

==French rugby union==
Vaealiki was released by Manly after round 7 of the NRL to enable him to take up the offer of a two-year contract with Top 14 French rugby union club, [[SC Albi]].<ref name="Wigan old boy heads for union">{{cite web|url=http://www.sportinglife.com/rugbyleague/news/story_get.cgi?STORY_NAME=rleague/08/05/01/RUGBYL_Vaealiki.html|title=Wigan old boy heads for union|publisher=Sportinglife|date=1 May 2008|accessdate=1 May 2007|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110605010332/http://www.sportinglife.com/rugbyleague/news/story_get.cgi?STORY_NAME=rleague%2F08%2F05%2F01%2FRUGBYL_Vaealiki.html|archivedate=5 June 2011|df=dmy}}</ref>

==International career==
Vaealiki earned international selection for the [[Junior Kiwis]] in 1998 and played for [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] between 2000 and 2003, including the [[2000 Rugby League World Cup|World Cup]] in 2000.
